method_atom: A semiclassical (WKB) treatment of a linear system evolving under a time-dependent control parameter, in which the amplitude for transitions between the asymptotic early-time and late-time mode bases is encoded in a linear (Bogoliubov) transformation. Because the WKB approximation is only locally valid while the transition amplitude requires global information, the connection across a sequence of turning points in the complexified time plane must be resolved via the Stokes phenomenon; when the driving profile carries fine sub-structure, interference among multiple turning points imprints characteristic oscillations on the output spectrum, with the interference phase depending on the internal statistics/symmetry of the excited modes.
object_atom: Electron-positron pair creation out of the QED vacuum subjected to a strong, time-dependent electric field with sub-cycle laser-pulse structure, computed for both spinor and scalar QED (and, more broadly, particle production from external electric, chromo-electric, or gravitational backgrounds).
brief_reason: The WKB/Stokes-phenomenon resolution of Bogoliubov asymptotic-state connections and its turning-point interference is the transferable machinery, while vacuum pair production in a structured laser field is the QED-specific object.
